meta.book: Lay, Linear Algebra and Its Applications

preambles.text: In each part, A represents an n×n matrix.

items.label: 1a
items.text: If A is orthogonally diagonalizable, then A is symmetric.

items.label: 1b
items.text: If A is an orthogonal matrix, then A is symmetric.

items.label: 1c
items.text: If A is an orthogonal matrix, then ‖Ax‖ = ‖x‖ for all x in R^n.

items.label: 1d
items.text: The principal axes of a quadratic form x^T A x can be the columns of any matrix P that diagonalizes A.

items.label: 1e
items.text: If P is an n×n matrix with orthogonal columns, then P^T = P^-1.

items.label: 1f
items.text: If every coefficient in a quadratic form is positive, then the quadratic form is positive definite.

items.label: 1g
items.text: If x^T A x > 0 for some x, then the quadratic form x^T A x is positive definite.

items.label: 1h
items.text: By a suitable change of variable, any quadratic form can be changed into one with no cross-product term.

items.label: 1i
items.text: The largest value of a quadratic form x^T A x, for ‖x‖ = 1, is the largest entry on the diagonal of A.

items.label: 1j
items.text: The maximum value of a positive definite quadratic form x^T A x is the greatest eigenvalue of A.

items.label: 1k
items.text: A positive definite quadratic form can be changed into a negative definite form by a suitable change of variable x = Pu, for some orthogonal matrix P.

items.label: 1l
items.text: An indefinite quadratic form is one whose eigenvalues are not definite.

items.label: 1m
items.text: If P is an n×n orthogonal matrix, then the change of variable x = Pu transforms x^T A x into a quadratic form whose matrix is P^-1 A P.

items.label: 1n
items.text: If U is m×n with orthogonal columns, then U U^T x is the orthogonal projection of x onto Col U.

items.label: 1o
items.text: If B is m×n and x is a unit vector in R^n, then ‖Bx‖ ≤ σ_1, where σ_1 is the first singular value of B.

items.label: 1p
items.text: A singular value decomposition of an m×n matrix B can be written as B = PΣQ, where P is an m×m orthogonal matrix, Q is an n×n orthogonal matrix, and Σ is an m×n "diagonal" matrix.

items.label: 1q
items.text: If A is n×n, then A and A^T A have the same singular values.
